at each inject path, what do we send, exactly, to the model?
Explained that there are two LLM calls (SELECT gate and COMPILE), detailing what system/user messages each receives, plus several short-circuit paths that reach no model at all.
I mean, what messages[] array do we send to the model? what are we providing it and asking from it?

Both calls are exactly two messages (system + user, no tools)—SELECT provides catalog+recent convo asking for catalog keys, and COMPILE provides line-numbered selected sources+recent convo asking for cited synthesis.

we should box the transcript in a way so that we are adding context only on what the user recently said -- since we are providing the running transcript we are injecting stuff that has already been injected before for prior messages from the user.

This is a more complex problem that apparent, because for example if the user says "What is the oauth support like?" we might inject some stuff, then if the user says "and do we support google as a provider?" we would need to a) know that the context from the previous question is "oauth" and that the new question is about a specific provider; so the new question adds some dimension to the question that narrows down what we actually need to search on.

how should we improve this?

Recommended two mechanisms folded into the existing two LLM calls: query resolution in the gate (emit a QUERY: standalone question line) and a per-session injection ledger for delta-compile (compile surfaces only new facts not already in context).
API connection error interrupted the session.
Implementation complete—query resolution folded into the gate and per-session ledger for delta-compile—but not yet validated against a live LLM due to missing API config.
run the validation with the ollama model -- its configured already

Validation passed end-to-end: gate correctly decontextualized anaphoric references and compile correctly suppressed already-injected facts, though the original 26b-mlx model always times out before completing the LLM path.

After a six-way model bake-off, set the config to gemma4:31b-cloud as the only model that both fits the 25s budget and passes all behaviors; the feature (resolve+dedup) is validated end-to-end.
